title: UPI Collect Request Scams
description: Learn how scammers use fake refund requests to steal money directly from your bank account.

lessons.title: The Fake Refund

steps.text: You receive a call from someone claiming to be from PhonePe or Paytm customer support. They say a previous transaction failed and they want to refund your money.
They tell you to open your UPI app. Suddenly, a pop-up appears asking you to enter your UPI PIN.
The caller insists you must enter your PIN to receive the money.

redFlags: You NEVER need to enter your UPI PIN to RECEIVE money.
Customer support will never ask you to open your app and enter a PIN.
The pop-up actually says 'Pay' or 'Send', not 'Receive'.
lessons.protection: If someone tells you to enter your UPI PIN to get a refund, disconnect immediately and block the number.

title: Digital Arrest Scams
description: Scammers impersonate police or CBI to scare you into transferring all your savings.

lessons.title: The Fake CBI Call

steps.text: You receive a video call on WhatsApp or Skype. The person is in a police uniform, sitting in what looks like a police station.
They tell you your Aadhaar card or phone number was used to send illegal drugs or money laundering.
They say you are under 'Digital Arrest' and cannot disconnect the call. They demand you transfer all your bank balance to an 'RBI safe account' for verification.

redFlags: Police or CBI do NOT make official arrest calls via WhatsApp or Skype.
There is no legal concept of 'Digital Arrest' in Indian law.
Real law enforcement will never ask you to transfer your money to a 'safe account'.
lessons.protection: Cut the call immediately. Real police will come to your house with a warrant, not call you on Skype. Report the number to 1930.

title: KYC Update Scams
description: Scammers send urgent text messages claiming your bank account will be blocked unless you update KYC.

lessons.title: The Urgent SMS

steps.text: You get an SMS saying: 'Dear Customer, your HDFC account will be blocked in 24 hours. Click this link to update your PAN/KYC immediately.'
You panic and click the link. It takes you to a website that looks exactly like your bank's login page.
You enter your username, password, and the OTP you just received.

redFlags: Banks never send SMS messages with links to update KYC.
Urgent threats (like 'account will be blocked in 24 hours') are a classic scare tactic.
The website URL does not match the official bank URL (e.g., it might be hdfc-update-kyc.com instead of hdfcbank.com).
lessons.protection: Never click links in SMS messages. Always type your bank's URL directly into your browser or use their official mobile app. Forward suspicious messages to 1909.
